title: astragalus vesicarius subsp. carniolicus
intro: Astragalus vesicarius subsp. carniolicus, also known as the Carniolan milk vetch or Astragalus carniolicus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and diuretic effects, which aid in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Astragalus vesicarius subsp. carniolicus include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Astragalus vesicarius subsp. carniolicus is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, with the root of the plant being the most commonly used part for herbal preparations.
